growth_records.notes: growth_rate_per_h computed as ln(2)/doubling_time_h. Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.medium: Chemostat defined medium (as detailed in Bacon et al. 2004)
growth_records.cite: Sidders et al. Quantification of global transcription patterns in prokaryotes using spotted microarrays. Genome Biology (2007). DOI:10.1186/gb-2007-8-12-r265. [PMC2246267]
growth_records.snippet: Table 3 dataset 4: Wild-type Mbovis AF2122/97 aerobic chemostat (VLA, UK). Chemostat cultures ... maintained at 37°C, with a pH of 6.9 and a generation time of 24 hours.
growth_records.notes: Generation time 24 h, 37 C, pH 6.9 are stated in the general chemostat methods; steady-state mu = D = ln2/24 = 0.028881/h ~ 0.0289/h applied to the M. bovis aerobic chemostat dataset. Caveat: M. bovis chemostat run performed at a different institution (VLA) than the M. tuberculosis run (CAMR); the 24 h generation time may not be independently verified for this run -> confidence low. Medium composition not itemised.

up.method: measured (glycerokinase-coupled enzyme assay of supernatant vs fresh medium); numeric value reported only graphically in Figure 1, not in text
model constraint 0-0.04 mmol/gDW/h (not a direct measurement)

sec.method: CO2 evolution monitored for steady-state determination; no rate value reported

yields.Yxs: biomass production yield (reciprocal of slope of glycerol-uptake vs growth-rate line) reported to fall within 95% CI of experimental value; no numeric value given in text

growth_records.cite: Beste et al. GSMN-TB: a web-based genome-scale network model of Mycobacterium tuberculosis metabolism. Genome Biology (2007). DOI:10.1186/gb-2007-8-5-r89. [PMC1929162]
growth_records.snippet: Chemostat cultures were grown in Roisin's minimal medium at a constant dilution rate of 0.02 per hour (equivalent to a doubling time of 34.7 hours). ... M. bovis BCG strain (ATCC 35748) was cultured in a 2-l bioreactor under aerobic conditi
growth_records.notes: Chemostat steady state => mu = D = 0.02 /h. Doubling time reported directly as 34.7 h (consistent: td = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.02 = 34.66 h). Temperature not stated in text (null). Medium is Roisin's minimal medium (cited, full salt composition not given in text) with glycerol as growth-limiting carbon source plus Tween 80; medium_composition_stated=false. Glycerol uptake was measured (glycerokinase-coupled assay) but numeric value appears only in Figure 1 as points at three dilution rates, so rate left null. The three growth rates in Figure 1 derive partly from a prior report [ref 25]; the Methods of THIS paper give a single chemostat set point of D=0.02/h. Oleic acid transport 0-0.04 mmol/gDW/h and dilution rates 0.01 (td 69 h) / 0.03 (td 23 h) are in silico model settings, not experimental, so not emitted as records.

growth_records.cite: Cox, Robert Ashley et al. Adaptation of Mycobacteria to Growth Conditions: A Theoretical Analysis of Changes in Gene Expression Revealed by Microarrays. PLoS ONE (2013). DOI:10.1371/journal.pone.0059883. [PMC3625197]
growth_records.snippet: Table 3: BCG-Pasteur Chemostat carbon limited; Faster growth rate 0.03 h-1; Slower growth rate 0.01 h-1; Temperature 37 C. Footnote: growth conditions from [11] Beste et al.
growth_records.notes: Prokaryote chemostat condition. At chemostat steady state mu = D, so growth_rate_per_h = dilution_rate = 0.01 h-1 (Table 3, slower rate). doubling_time_h = ln2/mu = 0.693/0.01 = 69.3 h. Carbon-limited defined medium; full composition NOT stated in this paper. Growth conditions are cited FROM Beste et al. (ref 11), the primary microarray study; this paper is a theoretical re-analysis. No substrate uptake, product secretion, or MFA fluxes reported.

growth_records.cite: Cox, Robert Ashley et al. Adaptation of Mycobacteria to Growth Conditions: A Theoretical Analysis of Changes in Gene Expression Revealed by Microarrays. PLoS ONE (2013). DOI:10.1371/journal.pone.0059883. [PMC3625197]
growth_records.snippet: Table 3: BCG-Pasteur Chemostat carbon limited; Faster growth rate 0.03 h-1; Slower growth rate 0.01 h-1; Temperature 37 C. Footnote: growth conditions from [11] Beste et al.
growth_records.notes: Prokaryote chemostat condition, faster (near-maximum) rate; text notes a threefold change in growth rate for BCG-Pasteur. mu = D at steady state = 0.03 h-1 (Table 3). doubling_time_h = ln2/mu = 0.693/0.03 = 23.1 h. Carbon-limited defined medium; full composition NOT stated here. Growth conditions cited FROM Beste et al. (ref 11). No uptake/secretion/MFA rates reported.
